description_long: This object is a large, multi-barreled rotary machine gun, commonly known as a minigun or Gatling-style weapon. It features six parallel barrels arranged in a cylindrical cluster, with a robust central housing that likely contains the firing mechanism and motor. The rear section includes a prominent pistol grip and a secondary handle for stabilization, as well as a visible trigger assembly. Attached to the underside is a cylindrical component, likely a power supply or ammunition feed, and various cables and mounting brackets are visible along the body. The overall construction is metallic, with a dark, matte finish and some glossy highlights on the barrels and housing. The design is utilitarian and rugged, suitable for heavy-duty use in urban defense or vehicle mounting scenarios.
description: A large, six-barreled rotary machine gun with a robust metal body, pistol grip, and attached power or ammo unit. The weapon has a dark, matte metallic finish and a rugged, industrial appearance.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the six barrels, central housing, pistol grip, and attached cylindrical unit beneath.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular arrangement of the six barrels and the central axis of the weapon.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, with clear visibility of the barrels, housing, and grip.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the pistol grip, trigger assembly, and the back ends of the barrels.
